zh.lifeSummary: Paul Ward是Ralfe Warde的兒子，於1519年出生在英國肯特。他於1539年與Rio Chillham在英國英格蘭肯特爾哈姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1583年6月在英國英格蘭肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Paul Ward是Ralfe Warde的儿子，于1519年出生在英国肯特。他于1539年与Rio Chillham在英国英格兰肯特爾哈姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1583年6月在英国英格兰肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特斯塔普勒爾斯特哥吉拉 沙也 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Ward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a watchman or guard, from Middle English ward ‘watchman, guard’ (Old English weard, used as both an agent noun and an abstract noun).
English: occupational name from Middle English warde ‘armed guard’ (Old English weard ‘watching, guarding’), with the same meaning as 1 above.

Irish: shortened form of McWard, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Bhaird ‘son of the poet’. The surname occurs throughout Ireland, where three different branches of the family are known as professional poets.

variants.name: Warshawsky
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Ari, Hyman.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from the city of Warsaw (Polish Warszawa), which became the capital of Poland at the end of the 16th century, after the destruction of Kraków by fire.
Americanized form of Polish Warszawski, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Wardman
meanings: English (Yorkshire): occupational name from Middle English wardman, either in the general sense ‘watchman, guard’ (compare Ward ) or in the administrative sense ‘representative of a ward within a borough’.

variants.name: Warde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Cormac, Donal.

nameMeanings.name: Paul
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Paulus, a Roman family name, originally a nickname meaning ‘small’, used in the post-classical period as a given name. Pre-eminently this is the name of the saint who is generally regarded, with St Peter , as co-founder of the Christian Church. Born in Tarsus, and originally named Saul, he was both a Roman citizen and a Jew, and at first found employment as a minor official persecuting Christians. He was converted to Christianity by a vision of Christ while on the road to Damascus, and thereafter undertook extensive missionary journeys, converting people, especially Gentiles, to Christianity all over the eastern Mediterranean. His preaching aroused considerable official hostility, and eventually he was beheaded at Rome in about ad 65 . He is the author of the fourteen epistles to churches and individuals which form part of the New Testament. It has been in continuous use in the British Isles since the 16th century.

variants.name: Paulus
meanings: From Latin Paulus, a Roman family name, originally a nickname meaning ‘small’, used in the post-classical period as a given name. Pre-eminently this is the name of the saint who is generally regarded, with St Peter , as co-founder of the Christian Church. Born in Tarsus, and originally named Saul, he was both a Roman citizen and a Jew, and at first found employment as a minor official persecuting Christians. He was converted to Christianity by a vision of Christ while on the road to Damascus, and thereafter undertook extensive missionary journeys, converting people, especially Gentiles, to Christianity all over the eastern Mediterranean. His preaching aroused considerable official hostility, and eventually he was beheaded at Rome in about ad 65 . He is the author of the fourteen epistles to churches and individuals which form part of the New Testament. It has been in continuous use in the British Isles since the 16th century.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: Paul Ward was born in 1519, in Kent, England as the son of Ralfe Warde. He married Rio Chillham in 1539, in Chilham, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in June 1583, in Staplehurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in All Saints' Church, Staplehurst, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

Il est décédé en juin 1583, à Staplehurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à All Saints' Church, Staplehurst, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Paul Wardは1519年にイギリスケントでRalfe Wardeの息子として生まれた。1539年にイギリスイギリスケントチルハムでRio Chillhamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1583年6月にイギリスイギリスケントスタプレハルストで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントスタプレハルストアル サイントス' チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.All Saints' Church, Staplehurst, Maidsto: 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 스타플레후르스트 앨 세인츠' c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Paul Ward(은)는 1519년 영국 켄트에서 Ralfe Warde의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1539년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 치함에서 Rio Chillham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1583년 6월 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 스타플레후르스트에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 스타플레후르스트 앨 세인츠' c후르치에 묻혔다.

ru.All Saints' Church, Staplehurst, Maidsto: алл сеинц' чурч, стаплэурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Paul Ward родился в 1519 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. Его отца звали Ralfe Warde. Он женился на женщине по имени Rio Chillham в 1539 г. в местности чилхам, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер июнь 1583 г. в местности стаплэурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности алл сеинц' чурч, стаплэурст, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
